title: Crucible Casting
subtitle: Melt, pour, and cast metal parts

tutorial.title: Crucible Casting Tutorial
tutorial.subtitle: From raw ore to a cast part

objectives.label: Imprint a clay mould
substeps: Make a Clay Mold Base at the Pottery Slab.
Right-click the base with a wooden or stone tool (e.g. a Wooden Axe).
The mould takes that tool's shape. Fire it in a Kiln to harden it.

objectives.label: Melt ore in a crucible
substeps: Place a crucible in a lit Bloomery.
Right-click it with raw ore to add 50 mB per piece.
Hold the heat in the melt band until the charge turns molten.

objectives.label: Pour a casting
substeps: Carry the molten crucible to a fired mould.
Right-click the mould to pour the metal in.
Let it cool, then collect the cast head or blade.

pages.text: Bronze-age metal is &6cast&r, not hammered from a bar. Drop raw ore into a &6crucible&r — each piece adds &650 mB&r of molten metal — and heat the crucible inside a &6Bloomery&r until the charge turns liquid.

Carry the molten crucible to a &6fired clay mould&r and pour. Each mould needs a set amount: a knife takes 80 mB, an axe or pickaxe head 150 mB, a sword blade 200 mB. Pour only fills up to what the mould needs — leftover metal stays in the crucible to cool or top up.
Moulds start as a &6Clay Mold Base&r imprinted by a wood or stone tool, then fired in a Kiln. The cast part is a rough head or blade; finish it on the &6Stone Anvil&r.

pages.caption: Crucible, mould, ore, and the cast part
